summary: High‑energy second set with scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and a monster Not Fade Away > Morning Dew, captured on a solid soundboard tape.
review: The reviewers rave about the powerful second‑set flow – especially the blistering Scarlet Begonias into Fire on the Mountain and the unforgettable Not Fade Away into Morning Dew.  The soundboard source is clear enough to highlight Phil’s booming bass and the drummers’ tight work, making it a must‑listen for fans of the late‑1979 peak.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviews are 4–5 stars and praise both performance (standout songs, energy) and recording (clear mix, strong instrument separation).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix is clear with Phil’s bass turned up prominently; drums are well‑defined. No major dropouts or muffling reported.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is described as energetic and tightly locked in, with strong transitions that got the crowd roaring.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Crowd reactions to Jerry’s playing are called “huge chills,” indicating his lead work was thrilling.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is turned up loud in the mix and described as “all over the place” – a powerful, bomb‑dropping presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers receive praise for doing a very nice job, even from reviewers not usually into drum‑centric jams.
